_about: Locked challenge-commitment test vectors for KMS strict mode (#119): grant-session (final_hash) AND mint (label digest). SDK MUST recompute these byte-identically before flipping #63 strict. Verify with compute_vectors.py.

mint._spec: CREATE (create_agent_key / create_p256_session_key) binds the client-authorized label: mint_digest = SHA-256(tag || wallet_id[16 BE] || SHA-256(label)), tag = AA-AGENT-MINT-v2 / AA-P256-SESSION-MINT-v2. Agent-credential REFRESH binds the agent_index being re-minted under a DISTINCT tag: refresh_digest = SHA-256('AA-AGENT-REFRESH-v2' || wallet_id[16 BE] || agent_index[u32 BE]) — the distinct tag prevents a refresh assertion being replayed to create (or vice versa). challenge = base64url_nopad(SHA-256(nonce || digest)). Server-derived params (the allocated index for CREATE, subject, ttl) are NOT bound; ttl is bounded by MAX_AGENT_JWT_TTL=24h. is_refresh selects the branch; a compromised CA can't flip it (the client committed to the matching shape). Source: kms/ta/src/main.rs mint_label_digest / agent_refresh_digest (#115). User presence (#111) remains the critical protection.

grant._spec: inner = keccak256(abi.encode(string tag, uint256 chainId, address verifyingContract, address account, <address sessionKey | bytes32 keyX, bytes32 keyY>, uint48 expiry, address contractScope, bytes4 selectorScope, uint16 velocityLimit, uint32 velocityWindow, bytes32 callTargetsHash, bytes32 selectorsHash, uint256 nonce)). callTargetsHash = keccak256(abi.encodePacked(address[])) where Solidity pads EACH element to 32 bytes (address left-aligned: 12 zero + 20 bytes). selectorsHash = keccak256(abi.encodePacked(bytes4[])) where each bytes4 is right-padded to 32 (4 bytes + 28 zero). final_hash = keccak256('\x19Ethereum Signed Message:\n32' || inner). Source: kms/ta/src/main.rs build_grant_session_inner / build_p256_grant_session_inner + keccak_packed_addresses/keccak_packed_selectors (#112). inner cross-verified against `cast abi-encode` (empty arrays) and the SDK's live contract oracle (non-empty arrays).

empty_arrays._note: callTargets=[] selectorAllowlist=[] -> callTargetsHash = selectorsHash = keccak256('') = c5d2460186f7233c927e7db2dcc703c0e500b653ca82273b7bfad8045d85a470. (tight-pack and pad-32 agree for the empty array — this case never exposed the #112 bug.)

nonempty_arrays._note: THE #112 regression-lock case. callTargetsHash/selectorsHash use Solidity pad-32 packing (NOT tight 20/4-byte). final_hash cross-verified against the SDK's live on-chain SessionKeyValidator.buildGrantHash oracle.
